next_action: CEO/operator should review the ranked batches and explicitly approve, reject, or continue holding individual scopes; this triage does not authorize any work.
summary: Ranked the CEO blocker board into five local decision batches, highlighting three high-leverage batches while leaving every gate held.

triage_batches.decision_needed: Choose whether to approve only read-only validation/legal review, keep all holds, or reject the marketplace route.
triage_batches.why_it_matters: This lane has the most complete local product packet and the largest cluster of related gates.

triage_batches.decision_needed: Decide whether read-only rules/scope review is worth approving before any report-route work.
triage_batches.why_it_matters: Security routes can become payout-relevant, but submission and testing gates must stay strict.

triage_batches.decision_needed: Approve or hold read-only public page refreshes for paid-code and competition opportunities.
triage_batches.why_it_matters: These are low-commitment discovery gates that can refresh payout/rules evidence without public action.

triage_batches.decision_needed: Separate model-cost/API approval from browser/Grok research approval; keep both held unless exact scope is approved.
triage_batches.why_it_matters: Platform research can improve infrastructure, but model/API and signed-in browser gates need exact cost and action boundaries.

triage_batches.decision_needed: Decide whether read-only browser discovery is useful now or should stay parked behind higher-value batches.
triage_batches.why_it_matters: These can add leads, but current product/security/paid-code gates look closer to actionable proof.
